mnemonics: GATES: Gate A Truth Expression Swiftly. (For AND: All True; OR: At least One True; NOT: Opposite; NAND: Not AND; NOR: Not OR; XOR: eXclusive OR - only one True)
keyPoints: Combinational Logic outputs depend ONLY on current inputs (no memory).
They are built from basic logic gates (AND, OR, NOT, etc.).
They can be described and designed using Truth Tables, Boolean Expressions, and Logic Diagrams.
commonMistakes: Assuming output depends on previous inputs (this is Sequential Logic).
Forgetting to simplify a Boolean expression before implementation, leading to inefficient circuits.
Creating a circuit with a 'race condition' or hazard due to unequal propagation delays in gates.
